Question: Match the body part or structure with its function in movement
Answer:
1) Bones - a) Provide support and shape
2) Muscles - b) Produce movement by contraction
3) Joints - c) Allow movement between bones
4) Tendons - d) Connect muscles to bones
5) Ligaments - e) Connect bones to bones
6) Cartilage - f) Cushions joints and prevents friction
7) Skeleton - g) Supports body, protects organs
8) Spine/Vertebral column - h) Protects spinal cord and provides flexibility
9) Skull - i) Protects brain
10) Rib cage - j) Protects heart and lungs
11) Hinge joint - k) Allows bending and straightening (elbow, knee)
12) Ball and socket joint - l) Allows movement in all directions (shoulder, hip)
13) Voluntary muscles - m) Controlled consciously for movement
14) Involuntary muscles - n) Work automatically (heart, intestine)
15) Smooth muscles - o) Found in internal organs, help movement inside body
